description: Priority 2: Family Portal Enhancement - Backend migration, controller, and routes for a dedicated family portal with daily summaries, automated weekly wellbeing reports, photo gallery from activities, and messaging system between family and care home staff.
steps: Create backend/migrations/007_family_portal.sql with tables: (a) family_daily_summaries (id, care_home_id, resident_id, summary_date DATE, meals_summary TEXT, activities_summary TEXT, mood_summary TEXT, care_notes_summary TEXT, photo_urls TEXT[], generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, generated_by VARCHAR - 'ai'|'manual'), (b) family_weekly_reports (id, care_home_id, resident_id, week_start DATE, week_end DATE, report_content TEXT, wellbeing_score_avg DECIMAL, highlights TEXT[], concerns TEXT[], generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, sent_at TIMESTAMPTZ, sent_to_emails TEXT[]), (c) family_photo_gallery (id, care_home_id, resident_id, photo_url VARCHAR(500), caption TEXT, activity_session_id UUID REFERENCES activity_sessions, uploaded_by UUID REFERENCES users, visibility VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'family', created_at TIMESTAMPTZ), (d) family_portal_tokens (id, care_home_id, resident_id, contact_id UUID REFERENCES resident_contacts, token_hash VARCHAR(255), expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Add relevant indexes.

Create backend/src/controllers/familyPortal.controller.ts with functions: (1) getDailySummary - generates or retrieves a daily summary for a resident by aggregating care notes (non-private), meal data, activity participations, and wellbeing logs for the day. Uses AI to create a warm family-friendly narrative. (2) getWeeklyReport - generates weekly wellbeing report with highlights using runAiOperation. (3) listPhotos - returns photo gallery for a resident. (4) uploadFamilyPhoto - uploads a photo to the gallery. (5) getFamilyDashboard - returns overview for a specific resident suitable for family viewing (today's summary, recent photos, unread messages, latest wellbeing score). (6) sendFamilyUpdate - staff sends an update message to family. (7) generateWeeklyReports - batch generates weekly reports for all active residents.

Register routes in backend/src/routes/index.ts under the existing '// -- Family Portal' section (extend it): GET /family/dashboard/:residentId (isStaff), GET /family/daily-summary/:residentId (isStaff), POST /family/daily-summary/:residentId/generate (isStaff), GET /family/weekly-report/:residentId (isStaff), POST /family/weekly-reports/generate (isManager), GET /family/photos/:residentId (isStaff), POST /family/photos/:residentId (isStaff with upload middleware), POST /family/send-update (isStaff).
acceptance_criteria: Migration SQL file exists at backend/migrations/007_family_portal.sql
findings: All acceptance criteria met. Migration creates 3 tables (family_daily_summaries, family_weekly_reports, family_photo_gallery) with proper indexes and constraints. Controller implements 7 functions: getDailySummary, generateDailySummary, getWeeklyReport, generateWeeklyReports, listPhotos, uploadFamilyPhoto, getFamilyDashboard. Routes registered after existing family message routes. Used (req as any).file pattern to avoid adding multer type errors (pre-existing issue with missing @types/multer). Build produces 14 pre-existing errors only, none from new code.
